back_translation: Now regarding the matter that you all wrote to me about: "It is good for a man not to touch a woman."
But because there is much sexual immorality, let each man have his own wife, and let each woman have her own husband.
Let the husband give what is owed to the wife; and likewise also let the wife give to the husband.
The wife does not have authority over her own body; rather, the husband does. Likewise the husband also does not have authority over his own body; rather, the wife does.

Do not refuse one another, except when you have come to agreement for some period of time, in order to devote yourselves to prayer; and then live together again, so that Satan will not come to tempt you on account of your lack of self-control.
What I am saying I say as a permission, not as a command.
I myself wish that all people might be like myself; but each one has his own special gift from God: some are one way, and some are another way.
Now to those who have not yet married and to widow-women I say: it is good for them to remain in this state like myself.
But if they cannot control themselves, let them go ahead and marry; for marriage is better than being burned-up by passion.
To those who are already married, I command — not I, but the Lord — that the wife should not separate from the husband.
But if she has separated, let her remain as one not married, or be reconciled with her husband; and the husband should not divorce his wife.
To the rest, I myself say — I, not the Lord — that if any brother has an unbelieving wife, and she is content to live together with him, let him not divorce her.
And if any wife has an unbelieving husband, and he is content to live together with her, let her not divorce her husband.

Because the unbelieving husband receives sanctification through the wife, and the unbelieving wife receives sanctification through the brother. Otherwise the children of you all would be unclean — but in fact they are holy.
But if the unbelieving one departs, let him depart. The brother or the sister is not under bondage in cases like these. God has called us to live in peace.
Wife, how can you know whether you will save your husband? And husband, how can you know whether you will save your wife?
Whatever the case, let each person walk in the manner that the Lord has appointed for him, in the manner that God has called him. And I myself prescribe these things in every church.
Was anyone called when he had been circumcised? Let him not undergo reversal. Was anyone called when he had not been circumcised? Let him not be circumcised.
Circumcision is nothing at all, and uncircumcision is nothing at all; but the keeping of God's commandments — that is what is significant.
Let each one remain in the situation in which he was called.
Were you called when you were a slave? Do not be concerned about it. But if you are able to receive freedom, then take that opportunity.
Because the one called in the Lord while he was a slave is the freed one of Him; in the same way, the freeman who was called is a slave of Christ.
You all received (royal) redemption at a high price; do not become slaves of human beings.
All you brothers and sisters, let each one remain in the situation in which he was called, abiding with God.
Now regarding those who have not yet married, I do not have a command from the Lord; but I give my opinion as one who has received mercy from the Lord to be faithful.
Therefore I think that this is good because of the present hardship — that is, it is good for a person to remain as he is.
Are you bound to a wife? Do not seek release. Are you not bound to a wife? Do not seek a wife.
But if you do marry, you have not sinned. And if a virgin marries, she has not sinned. But those who marry in this way will have hardship in the flesh, and I myself wish to spare you all.
All you brothers and sisters, I say this: the time has been shortened. From now on, let those who have wives be as those who have none —
and those who weep, as those who do not weep; those who rejoice, as those who do not rejoice; those who buy, as those who are not owners —
and those who use the things of this world, as those who do not use them to the full; because the form of this world is passing away.
I wish you all to be people without anxiety. The unmarried man is concerned about the matters of the Lord — how to make the Lord well-pleased.
But the man who has married is concerned about the matters of the world — how to make his wife well-pleased.

And he has a divided heart. The single woman or the virgin is concerned about the matters of the Lord, in order to be a holy person both in body and in heart-mind. But the woman who has married is concerned about the matters of the world, how to make her husband well-pleased.

I say this for the benefit of you yourselves, not to throw a noose to overpower you, but to enable you to act appropriately and to devote yourselves to the Lord without anything coming to disturb you.

But if any person thinks that he is acting inappropriately toward his betrothed who is still a virgin, and if she is past the age for marriage, and the matter ought to proceed in this way — let him do as he desires; it is not any sin; let them go ahead and marry.

But the one who has firmly resolved in his heart, without any pressure — the one who has authority over his own desire, and has decided in his own heart to keep his betrothed-virgin as she is — that one will do well.
Therefore the one who marries his betrothed-virgin does well; and the one who does not marry does even better.
A wife is bound as long as her husband is still alive; but if her husband passes away, she is free to marry whomever she desires — only in the Lord.
But according to my opinion, she will be more blessed if she remains in this state. And I think that I myself also have the Spirit of God.
